cases.input: I need to start a new brachytherapy case for a 62-year-old female patient with cervical cancer FIGO stage IIB. She has completed external beam radiation therapy with 45 Gy in 25 fractions to the pelvis. The tandem and ring applicator has been selected. Walk me through the complete workflow from CT simulation through plan optimization and quality assurance checks before treatment delivery.
cases.description: Complete brachytherapy workflow for cervical cancer case

cases.input: A new resident is starting their first week in the brachytherapy suite. They need to understand the end-to-end process for a prostate high-dose-rate brachytherapy plan. Please provide a detailed description of each step from patient consultation and imaging through treatment delivery and post-treatment verification, including all critical safety checkpoints.
cases.description: Educational walkthrough of prostate HDR workflow for new residents

cases.input: A brachytherapy plan has been approved and the patient is being set up for treatment. Describe the complete patient-specific quality assurance workflow from the time the plan is transferred to the afterloader through the start of treatment. Include independent dose verification, applicator position verification, source strength confirmation, and emergency procedure readiness.

A brachytherapy center is implementing a new electronic health record system that will replace the current paper-based brachytherapy treatment documentation. Describe the complete workflow for migrating brachytherapy-specific documentation templates, training staff on the new system, validating data transfer accuracy, and maintaining treatment continuity during the transition period. What specific brachytherapy data elements must be preserved in the new EHR system?
cases.description: EHR migration workflow for brachytherapy documentation

cases.input: I need to prepare a complete brachytherapy treatment plan for a 62-year-old female with stage IIB cervical cancer who has completed EBRT 45 Gy in 25 fractions with concurrent cisplatin. Post-EBRT MRI shows residual disease. She has a tandem and ovoid applicator in place. Walk me through the complete workflow from applicator placement verification to plan optimization, including imaging, dose prescription, OAR constraints, and plan evaluation.

Our department is setting up a new HDR brachytherapy program with an Elekta microSelectron (Ir-192, 10 Ci), Varian Acuity TPS, and Nucletron applicators. Outline the complete QA program including daily, monthly, and annual checks, source exchange procedures, TG-59 compliance, and documentation requirements.

I need to prepare for tumor board discussing a 48-year-old woman with recurrent endometrial cancer (stage IIIC1 serous carcinoma, TAH/BSO 18 months ago, 6 cycles adjuvant carbo/paclitaxel). PET-CT shows FDG-avid 3.2 cm vaginal cuff recurrence with no distant disease. What treatment options should I present, including brachytherapy approaches, expected outcomes, dose considerations, and evidence to cite?

We are implementing a new electronic brachytherapy documentation workflow. Describe the complete documentation requirements for cervical cancer HDR brachytherapy from initial consultation through treatment completion, including mandatory elements, imaging requirements, plan approval workflows, safety check documentation, treatment delivery records, and follow-up documentation.

A 70-year-old male with locally advanced rectal cancer (cT3N1M0,距肛缘4 cm) completed neoadjuvant CRT 50.4 Gy with capecitabine. At 8-week MRI, there is a 1.5 cm equivocal residual area. He strongly prefers organ preservation. What brachytherapy options could be considered, and what would be the complete workflow including applicator selection, imaging, dose constraints, and follow-up?

We need to establish standardized peer review criteria for HDR cervical cancer brachytherapy plans using tandem and ovoids. Create the complete plan review checklist including imaging quality, contouring accuracy, DVH parameters, plan optimization quality, source position verification, fractionation appropriateness, and safety considerations.

I am a radiation oncology resident preparing for my first independent HDR brachytherapy treatment delivery. Walk me through the complete pre-treatment safety checklist from patient identification through source calibration confirmation, applicator verification, imaging, plan verification, treatment delivery, source retraction confirmation, and post-treatment documentation. Include critical safety checkpoints and failure protocols.

Our department needs a comprehensive informed consent process for HDR brachytherapy for cervical cancer. Outline the complete consent content including specific risks with incidence rates, acute and late side effects, alternative treatments, treatment schedule, post-treatment care, and the process for meaningful informed consent.

We are preparing for ACR radiation oncology accreditation. Provide a comprehensive checklist of all documentation needed including policies, physics reports, equipment calibration records, TPS documentation, staff credentialing, patient records, incident reports, and QI data. What timeframe and format should records be in?

A 45-year-old woman with stage IVA cervical cancer completed definitive chemoradiation (EBRT 45 Gy + cisplatin/5-FU + 4 fractions HDR 28 Gy to point A). She now has a 2 cm exophytic vaginal apex recurrence 14 months later. Her renal function recovered after bilateral ureteral stents, ECOG 1. Outline the salvage treatment workflow including restaging, re-irradiation dose constraints, treatment options, and surveillance.

I need to design a workflow for integrating MRI-guided adaptive brachytherapy (IGABT) into our cervical cancer program. We currently do CT-based HDR with tandem and ovoids, have access to 1.5T MRI, and plan to acquire MR-compatible Elekta ring applicator. Outline the workflow changes including applicator selection, MRI protocol, image fusion, adaptive planning, and transition timeline.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about incident_reporting. incident classification system for brachytherapy including applicator-related, source-related, planning errors, delivery deviations, patient ID errors, and radiation safety incidents. Include severity grading, reporting timelines, investigation procedures, and corrective actions.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about permanent_seed_implant. Complete patient workflow for 68-year-old male with T1cN0M0 Gleason 3+4=7 prostate cancer (PSA 9.2, volume 55 cc) for permanent seed brachytherapy. Include pre-operative assessments, planning approach, implant technique, post-implant CT, and dosimetric goals (V100 >95%, D90 >100%).

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about emergency_hemorrhage. Emergency response protocol for 200 mL vaginal bleeding with hypotension (BP 85/55, HR 110) during fraction 3 of 4 HDR brachytherapy for cervical cancer. Include immediate management, applicator management, treatment modification, transfusion, documentation, and remaining fractions.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about re_irradiation_interstitial. Treatment planning workflow for 52-year-old woman with 1.5 cm upper vaginal recurrence (FIGO I) after prior EBRT 50 Gy + HDR 24 Gy 8 years ago. Include surgical excision, pre-operative MRI, interstitial applicator selection, re-irradiation dose prescription, cumulative dose calculations, and post-operative care.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about QI_project. QI project to reduce treatment planning turnaround from 6 hours to under 4 hours for HDR brachytherapy. Include current state analysis, root cause identification, intervention strategies, metrics, PDSA cycles, data collection, and sustainability.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about palliative_bone. Complete workflow for MRI-guided adaptive brachytherapy for painful left ischial metastasis from renal cell carcinoma (2.8 cm, prior EBRT 30 Gy 6 months ago, ECOG 2). Include patient selection, imaging, applicator, re-irradiation dose, pain management, outcomes, and follow-up.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about electronic_system. Data architecture for electronic brachytherapy treatment record system including treatment fraction data, CT/MRI image storage, plan versioning, approval workflows, source tracking, cumulative dose reporting, and Aria integration.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about tumor_board. Design multidisciplinary brachytherapy tumor board including meeting format, presentation template, required attendees, pre-meeting preparation, decision documentation, quality metrics, and outcome integration.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about anal_boost. Complete treatment workflow for 60-year-old woman with 3.5 cm anal canal cancer (T2N1M0) including brachytherapy boost after EBRT 36 Gy with 5-FU/MMC. Include applicator selection, MRI planning, target delineation, dose, OAR constraints, and functional outcome assessment.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about Joint_Commission. Joint Commission radiation safety documentation for brachytherapy including radioactive material license, RSO responsibilities, sealed source accountability, personnel monitoring, area monitoring, leak test documentation, and emergency procedures.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about melanoma_brachytherapy. Brachytherapy workflow for 48-year-old woman with 2.5 cm vaginal melanoma (Breslow 4.2 mm) after radical vulvovaginectomy with positive margins. Include custom applicator design, melanoma-specific dose, immunotherapy interaction, and surveillance.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about training_program. Comprehensive physicist training program for brachytherapy including orientation, supervised practice requirements, competency milestones, documentation, radiation safety training, TPS training, source handling, and timeline to independence.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about penile_preserving. Complete interstitial brachytherapy plan for penile cancer (T2N1M0, 2.5 cm glans lesion) after neoadjuvant chemotherapy. Include applicator design, MRI protocol, target delineation, dose (39-42 Gy in 13-14 fractions BID), OAR constraints, and functional outcomes.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about clinical_trial. Standardized procedures for EMBRACE-III trial participation including eligibility verification, MRI protocol standardization, contouring conventions, dose reporting, data submission, QA requirements, and deviation handling.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about VBT_decision. Evidence-based decision-making for adjuvant VBT in 47-year-old woman with Stage IA2 endometrial cancer (Grade 1, <50% myoinvasion, no LVSI) post-TAH-BSO. Include PORTEC evidence, risk stratification, arguments for/against, and brachytherapy approach if elected.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about prostate_shared_decision. Shared decision-making framework for prostate brachytherapy including indications, alternatives comparison, 10-year outcomes, risks (urinary, ED, secondary malignancy), procedure description, post-implant safety, and decision aid tool.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about oropharyngeal_salvage. Role of brachytherapy in salvage for 59-year-old man with 2.0 cm retropharyngeal lymph node recurrence after definitive IMRT 70 Gy for HPV+ oropharyngeal cancer. Include re-irradiation considerations, applicator selection, surgical coordination, dose, and cumulative tolerance.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about RCA_near_miss. Root cause analysis for 3 brachytherapy near-misses (wrong applicator size, CT without applicator, dwell position discrepancy). Include RCA methodology, common root causes, corrective actions, system improvements, and monitoring plan.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about ovarian_vaginal. Brachytherapy workflow for 72-year-old woman with 4 cm ovarian cancer vaginal metastasis (platinum-sensitive, partial response to carbo/gem). Include rationale, applicator selection, dose, fractionation, IP chemotherapy integration, and evidence.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about patient_satisfaction. Design brachytherapy-specific patient satisfaction survey covering pre-treatment experience, treatment experience, recovery, follow-up, and overall satisfaction. Include validated PROMs (EORTC QLQ-CX24, EPIC), Likert scales, and QI integration.

Comprehensive brachytherapy question about ovarian_VBT_repeat. Treatment plan for 43-year-old woman with ovarian cancer vaginal apex recurrence (high-grade serous, R0 resection) after prior hysterectomy and EBRT 45 Gy 3 years ago. Include VBT evidence, applicator, dose, niraparib timing, and surveillance.

A 68-year-old retired teacher named Robert has been diagnosed with favorable intermediate-risk prostate cancer (Gleason 3+4=7, PSA 9.5, clinical stage T1c, prostate volume 38 cc). He is very active and concerned about maintaining his quality of life, particularly sexual function and urinary control. He is considering between HDR brachytherapy monotherapy and LDR permanent seed implantation. Walk through the decision-making process with him.
cases.description: Real patient: Prostate cancer treatment decision-making

cases.input: A 35-year-old woman named Aisha is 20 weeks pregnant and has been diagnosed with Stage IB2 cervical cancer. She and her husband desperately want to continue the pregnancy while receiving cancer treatment. The multidisciplinary team must balance fetal safety with maternal cancer treatment. Design a treatment approach that considers all aspects of this complex situation.
cases.description: Real patient: Pregnant cervical cancer treatment planning

cases.input: A 65-year-old man named James has a T3N0M0 anal canal squamous cell carcinoma. He has completed 54 Gy of concurrent chemoradiation with mitomycin C and 5-FU. The post-treatment MRI shows a good partial response with a 1.5 cm residual abnormality. The tumor board is discussing whether to recommend observation, completion surgery, or brachytherapy boost. Analyze the options and provide a recommendation.
cases.description: Real patient: Residual anal cancer treatment decision

cases.input: A 45-year-old woman named Priya has a Stage IIIA endometrial cancer (serous histology) and has undergone total hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. The pathology shows deep myometrial invasion and lymphovascular space invasion. She needs adjuvant therapy and the team is considering EBRT alone versus EBRT with vaginal cuff brachytherapy boost. Analyze the treatment options for this patient.
cases.description: Real patient: High-risk endometrial cancer adjuvant therapy

cases.input: A 72-year-old man named William has been diagnosed with a 5 mm Stage IA1 squamous cell carcinoma of the cervix with no lymphovascular invasion. He is asking whether he needs brachytherapy or whether surgery alone is sufficient. The gynecologic oncologist has recommended radical hysterectomy but is open to discussing brachytherapy as an alternative. How should this low-risk case be discussed with the patient?
cases.description: Real patient: Early-stage cervical cancer treatment discussion

cases.input: A 58-year-old woman named Linda has a 3 cm rectal cancer located 5 cm from the anal verge. She has completed neoadjuvant chemoradiation with 50.4 Gy and capecitabine. The restaging MRI shows a good response with the tumor downsized to 1.5 cm. The surgeon believes she may be a candidate for watch-and-wait management, but the radiation oncologist is recommending a brachytherapy boost. Analyze this clinical scenario and the decision-making process.
cases.description: Real patient: Rectal cancer post-neoadjuvant treatment decision

cases.input: A 82-year-old woman named Helen has a small vaginal cancer (1.5 cm, Stage I) and is being considered for brachytherapy. She lives alone, has mild cognitive impairment, and relies on her daughter who lives 45 minutes away for transportation. She also has osteoporosis and is on a bisphosphonate. Design a treatment approach that accounts for all of her practical and medical considerations.
cases.description: Real patient: Elderly vaginal cancer with practical challenges

cases.input: A 40-year-old man named Carlos has a penile cancer involving the glans penis (Stage T2). He is very concerned about sexual function and body image. The options discussed at tumor board include partial penectomy, brachytherapy, or Mohs surgery. He wants to understand the pros and cons of each option specifically in terms of sexual function outcomes. Provide a detailed comparison tailored to his concerns.
cases.description: Real patient: Penile cancer treatment options with sexual function focus

cases.input: A 60-year-old woman named Fatima has a recurrent cervical cancer after hysterectomy. The PET-CT shows a 3 cm pelvic sidewall recurrence adjacent to the left ureter. She has had prior EBRT of 50 Gy. The team is discussing re-irradiation with brachytherapy versus systemic chemotherapy. She is asking about the chances of cure versus palliation and what the treatment will be like. Address both the medical decision-making and the patient communication aspects.
cases.description: Real patient: Recurrent cervical cancer re-irradiation decision

cases.input: A 70-year-old man named Richard has been treated with HDR brachytherapy for prostate cancer and is 2 years post-treatment. He presents with new-onset rectal bleeding. He is worried about cancer recurrence. Evaluate the likely causes of his rectal bleeding, the workup needed, and how to counsel him while managing his anxiety about recurrence.
cases.description: Real patient: Post-brachytherapy rectal bleeding evaluation

cases.input: A 38-year-old woman named Amara has a Stage IVA cervical cancer with bladder wall involvement. She has completed 45 Gy of EBRT with cisplatin. The MRI shows good response with tumor shrinkage, but there is still residual disease extending into the bladder wall. The team is debating whether brachytherapy boost is feasible given the bladder involvement, or whether the patient should be offered pelvic exenteration. Analyze this complex clinical scenario.
cases.description: Real patient: Stage IVA cervical cancer treatment analysis

cases.input: A 62-year-old man named George has been treated with interstitial brachytherapy for a recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the oral tongue. He had prior surgery (partial glossectomy) and chemoradiation. The interstitial implant used 6 needles and the treatment was completed uneventfully. He now presents 2 weeks post-treatment with pain, swelling, and purulent discharge from the implant sites. Evaluate his symptoms and design a management plan.
cases.description: Real patient: Post-interstitial brachytherapy oral tongue infection

cases.input: A 75-year-old woman named Dorothy2 has a Stage IIA cervical cancer. She is a Jehovah's Witness and has stated that she will not accept blood transfusions under any circumstances. She needs brachytherapy with interstitial needle placement. Given the bleeding risk associated with interstitial procedures, discuss how to manage this patient safely while respecting her religious beliefs.
cases.description: Real patient: Brachytherapy in Jehovah's Witness patient

cases.input: A 50-year-old man named Ahmed has prostate cancer with a very large prostate (140 cc), Gleason score 4+3=7, PSA 15, and Stage T2c. He is interested in HDR brachytherapy as monotherapy but the large prostate size raises concerns about catheter placement and dose coverage. Evaluate his candidacy for HDR brachytherapy and discuss any modifications needed for his large prostate.
cases.description: Real patient: Very large prostate HDR brachytherapy candidacy

cases.input: A 67-year-old woman named Patricia has completed brachytherapy for cervical cancer and is 6 months post-treatment. She reports difficulty with vaginal dilator use due to pain and is concerned about vaginal stenosis. She is also experiencing depression related to her cancer treatment. Address both the physical (vaginal stenosis prevention) and psychological (depression, treatment anxiety) aspects of her post-treatment care.
cases.description: Real patient: Post-brachytherapy physical and psychological care

cases.input: A 55-year-old man named Michael has a recurrence of squamous cell carcinoma in the base of tongue after prior transoral robotic surgery (TORS) and adjuvant chemoradiation. The recurrence is 2 cm and confined to the tongue base. The ENT surgeon is recommending salvage surgery (total glossectomy), but the radiation oncologist believes salvage brachytherapy may be feasible. Analyze the options considering his functional outcome expectations (speech and swallowing).
cases.description: Real patient: Recurrent oropharyngeal cancer salvage decision

cases.input: A 60-year-old woman named Grace has a Stage IIB cervical cancer. She has completed 45 Gy of EBRT and is scheduled for brachytherapy. However, she develops a pulmonary embolism 2 days before the planned brachytherapy procedure. She is started on therapeutic anticoagulation with enoxaparin. The team must decide whether to proceed with brachytherapy or delay treatment. Analyze the risks and benefits of proceeding versus delaying.
cases.description: Real patient: Brachytherapy timing after pulmonary embolism

cases.input: A 45-year-old woman named Kim has a Stage II vaginal cancer treated with combined EBRT and brachytherapy. Five years after treatment, she develops a new primary breast cancer. She asks whether her prior pelvic radiation has any impact on her breast cancer treatment options, particularly regarding radiation therapy for the breast. Provide evidence-based counseling about the interaction between prior pelvic radiation and breast cancer management.
cases.description: Real patient: Prior radiation and second primary cancer interaction

cases.input: 52yo F, 4.5cm IIB cervical SCC, HPV16+, left parametrial to pelvic sidewall, 2cm left external iliac LN. Post-EBRT 45Gy/cisplatin with LN response. Tandem/ovoid in place. Hgb 10.2, Cr 1.1, ECOG 1. Comprehensive treatment plan including applicator modifications, dose, optimization, and follow-up.

68yo M, Gleason 4+5=9, PSA 22.5, T3b, 85cc prostate. Completed EBRT 50Gy/docetaxel. HDR boost planned. Large median lobe, IPSS 22, prior TURP 5y ago (20g). Complete plan addressing anatomy, urinary risk, and dose.

35yo F at 24 weeks gestation with 3.5cm IB2 cervical SCC. Wishes to continue pregnancy (delivery at 32 weeks). On neoadjuvant cis/gem. Comprehensive multidisciplinary plan including fetal protection and delivery planning.

45yo F with 3cm vaginal cuff recurrence (Grade 3 serous) after TAH-BSO + EBRT 45Gy 2y ago. BMI 42, HbA1c 8.2%, OSA on CPAP. Interstitial brachytherapy plan addressing comorbidities and technical challenges.

60yo M, recurrent Gleason 4+4=8 prostate after RP + EBRT 64Gy. PSMA PET: anastomotic + SV bed recurrence. CAD (recent stent, dual antiplatelet), DM, BMI 34. Salvage HDR plan with cardiac and bleeding risk management.

28yo F with 2cm IA2 cervical adenocarcinoma, cold knife conization with negative margins. Desires fertility preservation. Pre-conization MRI no residual. LVSI negative. 3mm DOI. Observation vs adjuvant VBT decision.

70yo F with 5cm cT3N1M0 rectal cancer (3cm from anal verge), post-nCRT with good response. Organ preservation with brachytherapy boost. COPD (FEV1 45%), CKD (GFR 35), PVD. Comorbidity management and plan.
42yo M with 2cm T1N0M0 anal canal SCC, complete response post-EBRT 45Gy/5-FU/MMC. Brachytherapy boost planned. HIV+ (CD4 650, undetectable on ART). HIV-specific considerations and monitoring.
55yo F with 3cm vaginal melanoma (Breslow 3.5mm) after excision with 0.5mm deep margin. Right nephrectomy 10y ago (solitary left kidney, GFR 55). Adjuvant interstitial plan with renal considerations.
65yo M, intermediate-risk prostate (T2b, 38cc). Permanent I-125 seed implant. Penile prosthesis, left hip replacement, bilateral hand arthritis. Positioning and procedural challenges.
48yo F with IIIC2 cervical cancer post-EBRT. Tandem/ring + interstitial needles. Day 2 post-placement: fever 38.5C, lower abdominal pain, WBC 12,500. Blood cultures pending. Emergency management.
72yo F with recurrent IIIB cervical cancer (4cm, right parametrium) after definitive CRT. Pelvic exenteration + IORT considered. EF 35%, DM, mild cognitive impairment. Complex management plan.
14yo F with 3cm pelvic RMS (bladder/prostate, stage III, Group III). Post VAC/VI with partial response. Brachytherapy boost, Tanner I, organ preservation. Pediatric-specific plan.
59yo M, recurrent HPV+ oropharyngeal cancer (2.5cm retropharyngeal LN) after IMRT 70Gy + TORS 2y ago. ORN of mandible, xerostomia. Salvage plan addressing prior complications.
45yo F with 2cm Stage I vaginal SCC. SLE on HCQ + prednisone. Definitive brachytherapy. Autoimmune considerations: flare risk, immunosuppression, infection monitoring.
58yo M, Gleason 3+4=7, PSA 12, T2c, 72cc. EBRT + HDR boost. Bilateral hip replacements, severe kyphoscoliosis. Positioning challenges and needle placement modifications.
55yo F with 2.5cm recurrent Grade 2 endometrial at vaginal cuff after TAH-BSO + VBT 21Gy/3fx 5y ago. Hypothyroidism, HTN, osteoporosis (T-score -3.0). Repeat VBT plan.
50yo F with IIB cervical cancer, posterior tumor 2mm from rectum. Tandem/ovoid in place. On warfarin for DVT (bridging to LMWH). Tight posterior margin and anticoagulation management.
70yo F with 2.5cm ovarian cancer vaginal cuff recurrence after R0 resection. Prior breast cancer (ER+ PR+ HER2-, on tamoxifen 8y ago). Adjuvant VBT plan with tamoxifen interaction.

42yo F with IB1 cervical adenocarcinoma after radical trachelectomy + PLND (nodes negative). Desires fertility (12 oocytes frozen). Bicornuate uterus with functional right horn. Fertility-preserving VBT.

60yo M, recurrent Gleason 4+5=9 prostate after RP + EBRT 66Gy. Anastomotic site recurrence. Parkinson's disease (tremor, bradykinesia, carbidopa-levodopa). Salvage HDR with neurological considerations.
48yo F with 4cm IIIC2 endometrial cancer (Grade 2 endometrioid). Post TAH-BSO + PLND. EBRT 45Gy/carbo. VBT planned. Renal transplant 8y ago (tacrolimus, mycophenolate). Immunocompromised management.

60yo M with 2cm T2N0M0 anal canal SCC, complete response post-nCRT. Liver transplant 5y ago (tacrolimus, mycophenolate). Colostomy from diverticulitis. Definitive brachytherapy with immunosuppression.

35yo F with IA2 cervical SCC (no LVSI, negative conization margins). Desires fertility. Bipolar disorder (lithium, controlled), hypothyroidism, BMI 38. Fertility-preserving VBT with psychiatric and metabolic comorbidities.

70yo M, recurrent Gleason 4+5=9 after RP + EBRT 66Gy. Anastomotic + bilateral pelvic LN (3 nodes). Mechanical aortic valve (warfarin), HTN, DM. Salvage HDR + abiraterone with anticoagulation management.

55yo F with IIB cervical cancer (6cm tumor). EBRT 45Gy + cisplatin completed. Tandem placed. On imaging, tandem perforated uterine fundus into cul-de-sac. Hemodynamically stable. Emergency management.

42yo M, T1cN0M0 Gleason 3+3=6, PSA 5.2, 32cc prostate. Focal hemi-gland brachytherapy. Tumor right peripheral zone mid-gland. Family history (father, brother). Anxiety, normal sexual function. Focal treatment plan.

55yo F with IB2 cervical SCC. EBRT 45Gy/cisplatin completed. Tandem/ovoid in place. Jehovah's Witness (no blood products). Hgb 8.5, symptomatic anemia. Outpatient treatment. Management without transfusion.
68yo M with 3cm Gleason 4+3=7 (T2c, 58cc). Permanent Pd-103 seed implant. Bladder augmentation (ileocystoplasty), suprapubic catheter, colostomy. Complex abdominal anatomy plan.
32yo F with 1.5cm Stage I vaginal clear cell adenocarcinoma (no DES exposure). Fertility-sparing brachytherapy. Normal ovarian function (AMH 3.2). Comprehensive fertility-preserving plan.

60yo M with 4cm cT3N1M0 rectal cancer (2.5cm from anal verge), post-nCRT with good response. Severe claustrophobia (no MRI), pacemaker (MRI-conditional), chronic constipation. Endorectal brachytherapy with CT-based planning.

45yo F with 3cm recurrent IIB cervical cancer (left parametrium) after RP + EBRT 50Gy 3y ago. Colostomy (diverticulitis), left nephrostomy (ureteral stricture). Interstitial brachytherapy with altered anatomy.

70yo F with 2cm recurrent Grade 1 endometrial at vaginal apex after hysterectomy + EBRT 45Gy 4y ago. Severe kyphosis (Cobb 65), COPD on O2, mild dementia (MMSE 22). Daughter has healthcare POA. Complex management.
52yo M, T1cN0M0 Gleason 3+4=7, PSA 9.8, 50cc. HDR monotherapy 36Gy/4fx. Spinal cord stimulator, DVT 2y ago (off anticoagulation), severe needle phobia. Device safety and anxiety management.

65yo F with 3.5cm IVA vaginal SCC (bladder base involvement). Renal transplant 8y ago (tacrolimus, mycophenolate, prednisone). Immunosuppression, skin fragility. Definitive chemoradiation + brachytherapy.

48yo M, recurrent HPV- oropharyngeal cancer (2cm surgical bed) after IMRT 70Gy + TORS 2y ago. Severe dysphagia (MDADI 45/100), PEG-dependent, bilateral vocal cord paralysis. Salvage interstitial plan.

55yo F, recurrent IIB cervical cancer (1.5cm left parametrium) after CRT. Crohn's disease (infliximab, remission), severe aortic stenosis (valve area 0.8cm), bilateral LE lymphedema. Interstitial plan with multiple comorbidities.
48yo F with IIB cervical cancer (6cm), EBRT 45Gy + cisplatin. Tandem/ring + 4 interstitial needles. Fraction 2 completed. AKI: creatinine 0.9→2.1, oliguria. Emergency management plan.

72yo F, recurrent Grade 3 serous endometrial (2cm vaginal cuff) after hysterectomy + EBRT 45Gy 3y ago. Severe neuropathy (taxane, walking aid), bilateral LE edema, recurrent UTIs. Interstitial plan with functional limitations.

60yo M, 3cm T2N0M0 prostate (Gleason 4+3=7, PSA 12, 50cc). Combined EBRT 46Gy + HDR boost 18Gy/2fx. Total colectomy (end ileostomy), neurogenic bladder (CIC 4x daily), peripheral neuropathy. Absent rectum plan.

48yo F, IIB cervical cancer (5cm), EBRT 45Gy/carboplatin (cisplatin intolerance). Tandem/ovoid in place. Residual 2.5cm, left parametrial. CINV history, anxiety (sertraline), GERD (omeprazole). Supportive care challenges.

70yo M, recurrent Gleason 5+4=9 (T3bN0M0) after RP + salvage EBRT 64Gy 5y ago. Anastomotic mass 2cm extending to bladder neck. Bladder diverticulum 2.5cm, ureteral stricture disease. Salvage HDR plan.

38yo F, IIIC1 cervical cancer (4cm), EBRT 45Gy/cisplatin. Tandem/ovoid in place. Olympic track cyclist. Iron deficiency anemia, left tibial stress fracture (healed 6mo ago), OCP use. Athletic performance concerns.
65yo M, 3cm Gleason 4+5=9 prostate (T3a, PSA 25, 75cc). EBRT 50Gy + HDR boost 26Gy/2fx. Prior APR 10y ago (colostomy), right inguinal hernia mesh. Altered pelvic anatomy plan.

52yo F, 2.5cm recurrent Grade 3 serous endometrial at vaginal apex after hysterectomy + EBRT 45Gy 3y ago. New breast cancer (ER+ PR+ HER2-, 1.5cm, node-negative). Treatment sequencing for two cancers.

45yo M, 2cm T1N0M0 penile cancer (glans) after Fournier gangrene (3y ago, scarring). Type 1 DM (insulin pump, HbA1c 7.2%), smoking history. Penile-preserving brachytherapy with wound healing concerns.

60yo F, 3.5cm IIIC1 endometrial cancer (Grade 2 endometrioid). Post TAH-BSO + EBRT 45Gy/carbo. VBT planned. Post-gastric bypass (BMI 35, from 48), IDDM (HbA1c 8.5%), OSA (BiPAP). VBT with altered anatomy.

75yo M, recurrent Gleason 3+4=7 (PSA 6.5) after RP + salvage EBRT 66Gy 6y ago. Solitary anastomotic recurrence. ESRD (dialysis 3x/wk), CAD (2 MIs, EF 30%), bilateral BKA. Wheelchair user. Salvage HDR with severe functional limitations.
32yo F, IA2 cervical adenocarcinoma with LVSI (conization specimen), negative margins. Desires fertility preservation. Observation vs adjuvant VBT decision framework with competing risks.

58yo M, T1cN0M0 Gleason 3+4=7, PSA 10.5, 42cc. Permanent seed implant. Bilateral hip replacements, severe lumbar stenosis (epidural q3mo), bilateral carpal tunnel (post-decompression L). Positioning challenges.

45yo F, IIB cervical cancer (4cm). EBRT 45Gy/cisplatin. Tandem/ovoid + 4 needles. Fraction 1 delivered. TPS software error: dwell times based on incorrect source strength (actual 10% weaker). Immediate response.

58yo F, recurrent high-grade serous ovarian cancer (platinum-sensitive, 20mo relapse). 3.5cm vaginal + 1.5cm peritoneal deposit. Post-6x carbo/gem with response. Debulking + IP chemo + VBT boost. Prior bowel obstruction.

42yo M, 2cm T1N0M0 oral tongue SCC after partial glossectomy, 1mm positive margin. Adjuvant interstitial brachytherapy. Alcohol use disorder (sober 5y, naltrexone), HCV (SVR), nicotine dependence. Anxiety about speech/swallowing.

70yo F, recurrent IIIB cervical cancer (2.5cm central) after CRT. Pelvic exenteration + IORT. Severe aortic stenosis (valve area 0.6cm, NYHA III), severe osteoporosis (T-score -4.0, 3 vertebral fractures), rivaroxaban. Complex pre-operative plan.

55yo M, 2.5cm Gleason 3+4=7 (T2b, 42cc). Permanent seed implant. Crohn's (ileocolonic, adalimumab, remission), prior small bowel resection (20cm ileum), reversed loop ileostomy, bile acid malabsorption. Plan with IBD.

48yo F, 2.5cm Stage I vaginal SCC. Definitive interstitial brachytherapy. BMI 45, IDDM (insulin pump, HbA1c 9.0%), severe OSA (AHI 45, CPAP), bilateral LE lymphedema, chronic pain (gabapentin, duloxetine). Multiple comorbidities plan.

65yo M, 3cm T2N0M0 Gleason 4+3=7 (PSA 12, 50cc). EBRT 46Gy + HDR boost 18Gy/2fx. Total colectomy for UC (end ileostomy), neurogenic bladder (CIC 4x daily), peripheral neuropathy. HDR with absent rectum.
45yo F, IIB cervical cancer (5cm). EBRT 45Gy/cisplatin. Tandem/ovoid placed. Latex allergy (anaphylaxis). Standard applicator has latex components. Alternative approach and emergency preparedness.

70yo M, 3cm Gleason 4+3=7 (T3a, PSA 15, 65cc). EBRT 50Gy + HDR boost 26Gy/2fx. Metastatic colon cancer (liver resected 8y ago, disease-free), T1DM (insulin pump, HbA1c 7.5%), cardiac stent (aspirin + clopidogrel). Multiple cancer and comorbidity management.

52yo F, 2.5cm Stage I vaginal SCC (posterior wall). Definitive interstitial brachytherapy. Severe claustrophobia (no MRI), latex allergy (anaphylaxis), mechanical mitral valve (warfarin INR 2.5), CKD (GFR 42). Multiple medical constraints plan.

I am treating a 68-year-old male with prostate cancer (Gleason 4+5, Stage T3b, PSA 35, node-positive on PET-CT). He has a history of Crohn disease with an end colostomy, moderate COPD (FEV1 52%), and a mechanical aortic valve requiring lifelong warfarin (INR target 2.5-3.5). Current INR is 2.8. He had a DVT 2 years ago on a previous anticoagulation regimen. He wants definitive treatment. Given his extensive comorbidities, what treatment approach would you recommend? How do I manage his warfarin during brachytherapy? What concurrent chemotherapy should I add? What are the competing mortality risks?
cases.description: Extremely complex patient with prostate cancer and multiple life-threatening comorbidities

cases.input: I am treating a 60-year-old male with a 4 cm anal canal squamous cell carcinoma (Stage III, T3N1M0). He has well-controlled HIV with CD4 count of 420 and undetectable viral load on antiretroviral therapy. He has a history of IV drug use (clean for 5 years) and hepatitis C (SVR achieved). He has moderate depression requiring sertraline. He has no prior radiation. Standard treatment is concurrent chemoradiation (Nigro protocol). Should I modify the standard radiation dose or fractionation given his HIV status? Should I consider brachytherapy boost? What concurrent chemotherapy would be appropriate? How should I manage his depression during treatment?
cases.description: Complex anal cancer in HIV-positive patient with psychiatric comorbidity

cases.input: I have a 72-year-old male with a 2 cm prostate cancer (Gleason 3+3, Stage T1c, PSA 6.0) who has end-stage renal disease on hemodialysis (3 times weekly). He has a well-functioning arteriovenous fistula in his left arm. He has a history of renal cell carcinoma treated with right nephrectomy 10 years ago. He is on warfarin for atrial fibrillation (target INR 2.0-3.0, current INR 2.4). He wants definitive treatment. Given his ESRD, warfarin use, and solitary kidney history, should I recommend LDR brachytherapy? How do I manage his warfarin and dialysis schedule around the procedure? What dose modifications are needed?
cases.description: Complex patient with ESRD requiring specialized brachytherapy approach

cases.input: I have a 60-year-old female with a 4 cm endometrial cancer (Grade 3, Stage IB) who is a professional opera singer. She had a total hysterectomy with BSO and pelvic lymphadenectomy (negative nodes). She needs adjuvant therapy. She is very concerned about vaginal stenosis and its impact on her singing career (she uses her pelvic floor extensively for singing technique). She wants to minimize treatment-related side effects. Should I recommend vaginal cuff brachytherapy alone or combined EBRT and brachytherapy? What fractionation would minimize vaginal side effects? What vaginal dilation protocol should I recommend?
cases.description: Patient-centered decision minimizing vaginal toxicity for occupational needs

cases.input: I am treating a 50-year-old female with a 4 cm vulvar cancer (squamous cell, Stage II, depth of invasion 5 mm, 2 mm margins after radical vulvectomy). She has a positive left inguinal sentinel lymph node (0.3 mm deposit). She has a history of morbid obesity (BMI 48, lost 30 kg with bariatric surgery 2 years ago, current BMI 35). She has residual redundant skin in the inguinal region. She has well-controlled type 2 diabetes (HbA1c 7.0%). She needs adjuvant therapy. Should I recommend adjuvant radiation to the vulvar bed and inguinal regions? What brachytherapy approach could address the vulvar bed? How does her body habitus affect radiation planning?
cases.description: Vulvar cancer with obesity affecting radiation planning

cases.input: I have a 70-year-old female with a 2 cm endometrial cancer (Grade 1, Stage IA) who has severe aortic stenosis (valve area 0.7 cm2, peak gradient 58 mmHg). She is not a surgical candidate due to her cardiac risk. Her cardiologist says she has a 5-year survival of approximately 40% from her cardiac disease. She wants definitive treatment. Should I recommend definitive brachytherapy alone? What fractionation would minimize treatment visits? What anesthesia considerations are important? Should I consider active surveillance given her limited cardiac life expectancy?
cases.description: Inoperable endometrial cancer with severe cardiac disease

cases.input: I am managing a 32-year-old female with a recurrent cervical cancer (Stage IIIB) after radical hysterectomy and adjuvant chemoradiation (50.4 Gy EBRT + cisplatin + 30 Gy brachytherapy boost completed 10 months ago). The recurrence is a 4 cm central pelvic mass with bladder invasion causing hematuria. She has bilateral hydronephrosis requiring nephrostomy tubes. Her performance status is ECOG 2. Her creatinine is 2.8 (baseline 0.8). She has lost 15 kg in the past 3 months. Given the extensive prior radiation, poor performance status, and renal impairment, should I recommend re-irradiation, systemic therapy, or best supportive care? What systemic options exist?
cases.description: Complex recurrence with poor performance status requiring goals-of-care discussion

cases.input: I am treating a 45-year-old female with a 3 cm breast cancer (Stage I, triple negative, BRCA1 positive) who had a lumpectomy with close margins (1 mm). She is very physically active and a competitive swimmer. She wants breast-conserving therapy and refuses re-excision. She is concerned about long-term radiation effects on her swimming performance. She asks about the comparison between whole breast radiation, accelerated partial breast irradiation with brachytherapy, and proton therapy. She wants the treatment with the fewest side effects and shortest treatment duration. Given her triple negative histology and BRCA status, which approach would you recommend?
cases.description: Patient-centered decision for active patient with high-risk breast cancer

cases.input: I am managing a 48-year-old male with a 5 cm prostate cancer (Gleason 4+4, Stage T3a, PSA 22) who has a history of severe opioid use disorder (in sustained remission for 3 years on buprenorphine/naloxone). He has chronic pain from degenerative disc disease requiring ongoing opioid management. He has well-controlled hepatitis C from his prior IV drug use (SVR achieved). He has depression treated with venlafaxine. He wants definitive treatment. Given his substance abuse history and ongoing opioid use, should I recommend LDR brachytherapy? How does his buprenorphine/naloxone affect treatment? Should I adjust his pain management during brachytherapy? What are the implications for treatment compliance?
cases.description: Complex patient with substance abuse history and chronic pain requiring specialized management
